mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in consumer credit growth signals a supply-demand mismatch that triggers inventory drawdowns, boosting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained capacity utilization above 80 percent, rising industrial output directly translates to higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5 percent month-over-month for three consecutive months, contradicting the revenue transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in new housing starts reflects rising household confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically precedes positive equity market performance over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative predictive rank correlation over a 5-year rolling window, or housing starts rise while SPX 5-day forward returns decline in more than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the core consumer price index rises above 3.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL growth exceeding 3.5 percent for two consecutive quarters, indicating the inflation-valuation channel has broken down.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with stagnant real wages indicates a labor cost shock that erodes profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, surging consumer credit balances directly fund discretionary spending, driving positive corporate earnings revisions and lifting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2.0 percent month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, breaking the credit-spending transmission channel.
